meta.reportLabel: Wed Report — into PCE; DXY new 13-mo high ~101.4, AUD<0.70 despite firm trim-mean CPI 3.6%
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong / yen-weak, extending. Risk aversion + a fresh Fed-hike repricing wave lifted DXY to a new 13-mo high ~101.4 and crushed AUD over 1.2% through 0.70 to ~0.6915 — even as Australia's May trimmed-mean CPI rose to 3.6%. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near 1986 low); intervention coordination-flavoured after the Katayama-Bessent call. Everything funnels into Thursday's US PCE.
meta.nextBigEvent: US PCE — Thu Jun 25 (the marquee test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call)

dailyRead: The dollar-strong / yen-weak regime not only held but extended on Wednesday: risk aversion plus a fresh wave of Fed-hike repricing drove DXY to ~101.4 (a new 13-month high) and crushed the AUD over 1.2% through 0.70 to ~0.6915 — even though Australia's own data was, if anything, firm. The morning's marquee local print, Australia's May CPI, was a two-way: headline cooled to 4.0% (from 4.2%), but the RBA-relevant trimmed mean ROSE to 3.6% (from 3.4%) — a sticky core that keeps the RBA's 'risks-up' floor intact, yet the currency was swept under the dollar tide anyway. The cleanest trades remain the USD-divergence legs (EUR/USD short, EUR/JPY short) and the structural long-JPY core (USD/JPY short, JPYBASKET long) — but the JPY book is squeezed near a 1986 low (~161.5) with US-Japan intervention now coordination-flavoured, so defend, don't add. The dominant forward theme is singular: everything funnels into Thursday's US PCE, the one print that can fade a very crowded dollar — Tuesday's muted US services PMI is the only crack under it.

strength.drivers: Core PCE 3.3% (Apr); CPI 4.2%; claims ~229k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Regime extending: median '26 dot ~3.8%, easing bias dropped; DXY new 13-mo high ~101.4 on Fed repricing + risk-off. PCE Thu the only near-term fade — a soft core (Tue's muted services PMI hints) bounces a crowded long.

strength.drivers: Core sticky; CPI 2.8% (rising into Q4). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes) — but UK flash services CONTRACTED (first since Apr 2025).
strength.forward: 🦅→ Hawkish 7-2 BoE floor holds the rate story, but the UK services contraction re-opens the cut-tail under the rate-bid GBP. Watch the GBP legs. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: May CPI: headline 4.0% (from 4.2%), trimmed mean ROSE to 3.6% (from 3.4%); unemp ~4.5%. RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, minutes 'risks to the upside'.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim arms an Aug-hike case, but risk-off + Fed repricing crushed AUD/USD below 0.70. A soft US PCE flips the USD leg and the firm CPI bites. Jobs Thu. Next RBA Aug.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; PPI hot. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16), Summary of Opinions favours more hikes; spot ~161.5 (near 1986 low).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. Katayama-Bessent call signals US-Japan COORDINATION readiness → intervention risk two-sided and joint-flavoured, the swing. Japan CPI Thu. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: Headline easing; recovery establishing. RBNZ 2.25%; Q1 GDP firm +0.8% q/q (1.5% y/y). Markets price a 25bp hike Jul 9.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Firm Q1 GDP + a priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps NZD/USD. Net firm-but-capped into Jul 9 RBNZ.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (from 2.8%), gas +33.2%, food +4.3%; but trimmed core ~2.0%, median ~2.1%. BoC 2.25% (parked Jun 10), looking through energy.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor; oil at a 3-mo low (Brent ~$77) re-presses the export side; BoC looks through energy. Net less heavy than Sunday. Next BoC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.5% ('26 proj); headline 3.0% '26 proj; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11), framed as one-off war insurance, not a cycle; growth cut to 0.8%.
strength.forward: ⚖️↘ Pure USD-leg: hawkish Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish-frame ECB caps bounces. EZ PMI downturn easing ≠ a turn. Next ECB late Jul.

symbols.why: Cleanest macro divergence: hawkish-Fed (dots->hike, DXY 13-mo high) vs an ECB that framed its hike as one-off war insurance.
symbols.risk: Crowded into PCE — a soft core print (Thu) bounces it; trail stops.

symbols.why: Weak yen HELPS this: structural BoJ-tightening destination + a capped, dovish-frame EUR — cleaner than the USD/JPY leg.
symbols.risk: A coordination-flavoured intervention spike snaps every XXX/JPY lower at once.

symbols.why: Oil at a 3-mo low (~$77 Brent) re-presses CAD's export side; broadening CPI only a floor as BoC looks through it.
symbols.risk: Oil re-spike (MOU stall / Lebanon) rescues CAD; a soft US PCE caps the USD leg.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but SQUEEZED ~161.5 (1986 low). Defend, do NOT add here.
symbols.risk: Tokyo silent + clean break = momentum higher; a JOINT intervention spike is the violent re-load.

symbols.why: Cleanest commodity split: sticky AUD trim mean (3.6%) + RBA floor vs oil-pressed, look-through-CPI CAD.
symbols.risk: Risk-off hits AUD beta harder; a CAD-supportive oil bid; soft Aussie jobs Thu.

symbols.why: Structural-JPY destination vs a CAD capped by 3-mo-low oil; firmer Canadian CPI only a mild headwind to the short.
symbols.risk: Oil/gold bid firms CAD; an intervention spike snaps it lower abruptly (the re-load).

symbols.why: USD headwind dominant: risk-off + Fed repricing drove it 1.2% through 0.70 to ~0.6915, overpowering the firm trim-mean CPI.
symbols.risk: The sticky 3.6% core + a soft US PCE flips the leg — the firm-AUD invalidation.

symbols.why: Hawkish 7-2 BoE floors GBP, but UK services contraction re-opens the cut-tail under a bid dollar — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: Levels-only into PCE; soft UK data leans it short again.

symbols.why: Firm Q1 GDP + priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps it — net neutral into Jul 9.
symbols.risk: Risk-on lifts NZD; a soft US PCE or hot data helps.

symbols.why: UK services contraction took firmness out of the GBP leg vs the structural yen destination.
symbols.risk: Firm-rate GBP still fights it; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak yen leans it down, but risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor make it modest only.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — carry offsets, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.why: ECB done-dovish vs a hawkish-hold RBA (sticky trim mean) — mild AUD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: Aussie jobs Thu vs EZ data set direction.

symbols.why: ECB spent vs an NZD floored by firm GDP + Jul-hike pricing — slight NZD tilt.
symbols.risk: NZD risk beta or a late-Jul ECB shift.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: hawkish BoE vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK cut-pricing vs Aussie jobs split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish BoE firmed GBP vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — both firm, no divergence.
symbols.risk: Lowest conviction; trade levels only.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc, both floored (RBA sticky-core vs RBNZ Jul-hike priced) — a wash.
symbols.risk: Aussie jobs Thu vs RBNZ Jul 9.

symbols.why: Firm NZD (GDP + Jul hike) vs dovish-parked, oil-pressed BoC — mild NZD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: Oil-led disinflation undercuts NZD; an oil bid lifts CAD.

symbols.why: The strong leg: new 13-mo high ~101.4 (+0.4% Wed) on Fed-hike repricing + risk-off; EUR/JPY/GBP legs all soft vs USD.
symbols.risk: PCE Thu is the swing — a soft core or risk-on wave fades a stretched, crowded long.

symbols.why: Squeezed leg: basket fell as the yen hit a 1986 low — core only, do NOT add into a re-bid USD. (Rises when JPY strengthens.)
symbols.risk: Wait for a coordinated intervention or a PCE-led USD fade to re-load; thesis intact, timing wrong.

symbols.why: Oil-lower tailwind (Brent ~$77) + EZ PMI downturn easing; hawkish-Fed rates the cap, cancelled talks/Lebanon keep the tail wider.
symbols.risk: An MOU breakdown / Lebanon escalation gaps it down; a hawkish-Fed rates scare.

catalysts.note: Headline 4.0% (cooled), trimmed mean 3.6% (UP from 3.4%) — sticky core keeps the RBA floor; AUD still swept under the dollar.
Direct test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call; Tue's muted US services PMI hints a soft core is possible — the dollar's only near-term fade. Median '26 PCE 3.6%, core 3.3%.

catalysts.event: Australia jobs · Japan CPI · final US GDP
catalysts.note: AUD labour swing; JPY pipeline check; US growth read.
25bp hike priced; firm Q1 GDP backs it — the NZD swing.
USD/JPY ~161.5; Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed coordinated-action readiness. Biggest near-term swing for the whole JPY book, now joint-flavoured.
Clock ticking; the Burgenstock technical round stays cancelled (Iran tying it to a Lebanon ceasefire). Oil-respike tail a notch wider.
All next decisions parked to late July.

geopolitics: Flows still beat diplomacy on price. Washington's 60-day Iranian oil license plus 30M+ barrels shipped through Hormuz in the past week, with Gulf producers (Kuwait, ADNOC) restoring supply, pushed Brent to ~$77 and WTI to ~$74 (a fresh ~3-month low). The diplomatic track stays stalled: the Burgenstock technical talks remain cancelled, with Iran tying any resumption to an Israeli halt in the re-erupted Lebanon fighting. The 60-day MOU clock keeps running, so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact — but the slipped round plus the Lebanon flare keep the oil-respike tail a notch wider. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps the dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD on calmer days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (Warsh marked inflation up into the oil drop); for CAD the broadening CPI partly offsets the renewed oil drag → firmer-but-capped. Tail (a notch wider): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,195 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
